home.why_morocco_text: Ani celebrates the soul of her country, where heartbeats resonate in harmony with those of its people. Each painting is a pulse a heartbeat suspended between joy and melancholy, lightness and complexity, light and darkness.
home.serie1_p1: This triptych captures the contrasts of the ochre city. Klein and Majorelle blue evokes the Majorelle Gardens, a haven of calm and contemplation.
home.serie1_p2: Orange reflects the warm light on the ramparts, and Soulages black, dense and subtle, recalls the ancient doors, starry nights, and the mysteries of the Medina.

about.section1_p1: Ani is a French-Moroccan artist, sharing her life between Paris, Casablanca, and Geneva. Since childhood, drawing has always been a natural, almost instinctive language for her, inherited from her father, an art lover. This heritage has established itself over time as a solid foundation of her creative identity.
about.section1_p2: But it was following a personal ordeal, marked by heart problems, that gave a new dimension to her practice: transforming it into a vital necessity, a way to express and sublimate what words could not contain.
about.section2_p1: Ani chose to paint, to make vibrate on large canvases what constitutes the most precious thread of any existence: the heartbeat. The electrocardiogram became the matrix of her pictorial language, a vibrant texture that runs through her work. She varies its rhythms according to her emotions: long or short beats, fine or thick strokes, dark or bright. Thus, her paintings convey the truth of life in its contrasts, its phases of momentum and its moments of fall.
about.section2_p2: Her very first painting, made three years ago for her eldest sister, was a revelation. Since that day, she has never put down her brush. Her works have been selling continuously for over a year through her online gallery and social networks, reaching an international audience sensitive to her singular approach.
about.section2_p3: The first collection, 'Morocco Collection', is a tribute to her homeland. Each canvas illustrates the uniqueness of a city, its memory, its culture, and its heritage. Through this cycle, Ani summons her childhood memories and interweaves them with her present, staging in her canvases a space-time where France and Morocco intersect.
about.section3_p1: In her works, space becomes a place of vibration, movement, and shimmer. The precious materials she uses such as gold leaves, suspended without being fixed create their own autonomy, illustrating the fluidity and metaphor of the 'golden heart'. Combined with the reflections of colored glass, they transform light and endlessly renew the appearance of her canvases according to the surrounding air and breath.
about.section3_p2: The artist deliberately blurs the lines: she refuses the viewer the comfort of a simplified reading of History or society. She prefers to create a sequence of emotions, a succession of reminiscences and introspections, an exaltation of the senses that compels us to embrace the world in both its lightness and its complexity.
about.section3_p3: Her works are not merely seen: they breathe, they pulsate. In painting the heart, Ani reconciles spirit and intimacy. Since a painting is perfect when it comes alive in the way you read it, she leaves you free to project your own experiences onto her canvases.
about.services_p1: An art rental service is offered to companies wishing to enrich their workspace. This choice allows for regular renewal of artworks and support for contemporary art.
about.services_p2: Clients benefit from tax advantages and are accompanied by our specialized tax lawyers.

gallery.serie2_medium:
Knife acrylic · Klein Blue · Bright Orange · Soulages Black
Paris · The Muisca Gallery · 2025
gallery.serie1_medium:
Knife acrylic · Gold · Ochre · Colors of Moroccan cities
Paris · Casablanca · 2023–2024
gallery.color1_text: Dense, almost tangible a light for meditation and pure energy.
gallery.color2_text: The warmth of Marrakech's ramparts, the golden light of the Sahara desert.
gallery.color3_text: Subtle and dense, it recalls ancient doors, starry nights, and the mystery of the Medina.
gallery.artwork_aftertea_desc: Majorelle blue, earthy orange ochre, Soulages black. The Moroccan tea ritual extended into meditation.
gallery.artwork_theend_desc: The end not as silence, but as transformation — a rhythm that resonates through light, shadow, and emotion.
gallery.artwork_reminiscence_desc: A circle unfolds from the center — the blue becomes a dense, almost tangible light, a space for meditation.
gallery.artwork_dyptique_desc: Blue and orange oppose each other. The central line marks the boundary between these two breaths and two worlds.
gallery.artwork_casablanca_desc: The most sold and reproduced of the series. Casablanca marks the beginning of Galerie Ani's journey.
gallery.artwork_tanger3_desc: Green, symbol of healing, heritage, and rooted identity, runs through this painting.
gallery.artwork_marrakech_desc: Klein and Majorelle blue, orange of the ramparts, Soulages black — the contrasts of the ochre city.
gallery.artwork_tanger2_desc: Tangier, crossroads between continents and empires, carries the weight of centuries in its silence.
gallery.artwork_verticale_desc: Oil on canvas, 2026. The verticality of the gaze facing the immensity of the Sahara — Merzouga as a suspension point between sky and sand.

mentions.ip_text: The person responsible for the visual identity, development, design, and website requires that their name be mentioned next to each use of their works. Failure to credit the author constitutes an infringement of the author's moral rights under Article L. 121-1 of the French Intellectual Property Code.
mentions.credits_dev_detail: The website was developed in close collaboration with the artist Anissa Hafidi, whose artistic direction is rooted in a refined aesthetic, a palette of warm and neutral tones, and a particular sensitivity to light and texture. Every design decision fine typography with generous spacing, a dark background that lets the works breathe, minimalist navigation was made to step aside and give the paintings full presence on screen.

privacy.data_p1: The Gallery places great importance on the protection of privacy and personal data. In accordance with European Regulation 2016/679 of 26 April 2016, the French Data Protection Act of 6 January 1978 as amended by Ordinance No. 2018-1125 of 12 December 2018 and the Decree of 1 August 2018, the Gallery makes strong commitments to the persons concerned.
privacy.collected_intro: The Gallery collects personal data transparently and explicitly:
privacy.collected_li1: — Data expressly requested on the Site, including in particular data from contact / review / payment forms (last name, first name, email address, postal address and phone number)
privacy.purpose_p1: Each data collection is carried out specifically for explicit purposes clearly indicated on the page of the Site where the data is collected. With regard to the contact form and the payment page, the purpose pursued is establishing contact. The legal basis for this processing is the performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures (Article 6.1.b of the European Regulation).
privacy.purpose_p2: There is no fully automated decision-making by the Gallery based on your personal data.
privacy.recipients_intro: The recipients of the data are:
privacy.recipients_li1: — Ms. Anissa HAFIDI and the Gallery staff
privacy.recipients_li2: — Subcontractors and partners for the execution of the relevant processing
privacy.recipients_p: Personal data transmissions with recipients are carried out securely and pursuant to an agreement between the Gallery and each recipient.
privacy.retention_p1: The Gallery stores your personal data with a subcontractor whose servers are located in France, thus guaranteeing adequate protection under European Union law. The retention periods are as follows:
privacy.retention_li2: — Customer data: for the duration of the relationship and at most up to 5 years after its end
privacy.rights_intro: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.rights_li1: — Right of access to your personal data
privacy.rights_li2: — Right of rectification if it is inaccurate or incomplete
privacy.rights_li3: — Right to restriction (Article 18 of the European Regulation)
privacy.rights_li4: — Right to erasure (unnecessary data, withdrawn consent, unlawful processing)
privacy.rights_li5: — Right to object on legitimate grounds
privacy.rights_li7: — Right to define directives regarding the fate of your data after your death
privacy.rights_li8: — Right to withdraw your consent at any time
privacy.rights_contact: To exercise these rights, provide proof of identity and contact:
privacy.rights_complaint: In case of complaint, you may contact the
privacy.rights_bloctel: or register on the telephone canvassing opposition list at
privacy.cookies_p1: When you visit the Site, a cookie may be automatically installed on your browser. A cookie is a block of data that does not identify users but serves to record information relating to navigation.
privacy.cookie1_text: Record information entered in contact and payment forms. Necessary for the website to function, they cannot be disabled.
privacy.cookie2_text: Establish statistics on the number of visits and use of services in order to improve the ergonomics of the site. Can be configured in browser options.
privacy.cookie3_text: Issued by social networks (LinkedIn, Instagram) when using sharing buttons. The Gallery limits their presence as much as possible but cannot control their collection process.
privacy.cookies_p2: Analytical cookies have a lifespan of 13 months. You can disable them at any time via your browser settings. For more information:
